title: NOC 21100 – Physicists and Astronomers | GTR Immigration
description: Physicists and Astronomers drive scientific discovery, advanced technology development, and space research across universities, government laboratories, and high-tech industries in Canada. Classified under NOC 21100, this TEER 1 professional occupation continues to see growing national demand—creating strong immigration opportunities through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Design and conduct experimental and theoretical research
Analyze research data and prepare scientific reports
Develop industrial, medical, or laboratory instrumentation
Conduct celestial surveys and observational studies
Build numerical models of cosmic systems
Develop instrumentation and software for telescopes
Analyze cosmic radiation and astrophysical data
employment_requirements: Master’s or doctoral degree in physics or engineering physics
Master’s or doctoral degree in astronomy (for astronomer roles)
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate approximately 10,100 new positions for Physicists and Astronomers, while around 9,600 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and advanced-degree immigration planning especially valuable for overseas professionals.
additional_information: Physicists and Astronomers remain in demand across aerospace manufacturing, clean-energy research, telecommunications, medical imaging, and university-led scientific programs nationwide. Hiring has expanded steadily since the pandemic and is expected to continue over the coming years.
Aerospace and electronics manufacturersTelecommunications companiesPower utilitiesGovernment research laboratoriesUniversities and hospitalsScientific consulting firms
Growth in aerospace and satellite technologyExpansion of clean-energy researchMedical-physics and imaging innovationGovernment-funded science programsAdvanced-manufacturing development
Annual salaries typically range between $69,100 and $134,300 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and academic or industrial experience.
